data.title: Write behavioral replacement tests for replace-classified Python tests in tests/skills/
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Description
Read the triage classification from story ca06-e969's ticket comments. For each Python test file in tests/skills/ classified as "replace":

1. Identify what behavior the original test was indirectly verifying (e.g., which script, tool, or workflow step)
2. Write a behavioral replacement test that uses subprocess.run to execute the script/tool and asserts on exit codes, stdout, stderr, or file side effects
3. Name the replacement test to be fuzzy-match compatible with the source file it tests
4. Verify the replacement test passes

Use pytest with subprocess calls and tmp_path fixture. Do NOT use tests/lib/markdown_helpers.py (it is part of the anti-pattern). Do NOT grep source files in assertions.

## TDD Requirement
TDD exemption: Criterion 2 — replacement tests verify existing behavior (scripts/tools already work). No new source behavior is introduced. The task restructures test verification strategy without altering observable source behavior. The replacement tests themselves ARE the behavioral verification deliverable.

## File Impact
- tests/skills/*.py (replace-classified files — CREATE replacements)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] ruff format --check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff format --check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] Each replacement test executes code (subprocess.run or function call) and asserts on output, not source file content
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ! grep -rn "open.*SKILL.md\|open.*\.md.*read\|Path.*read_text.*md" tests/skills/test_*_behavioral.py 2>/dev/null; exit 0
- [ ] All replacement tests pass independently
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && python3 -m pytest tests/skills/ -x -q --tb=short
